channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The Signal attack plan messages: What we do (and don't) know
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The revelation that President Donald Trump's most senior national security officials posted the specifics of a military attack to a chat group that included a journalist hours before the attack took place in Yemen has raised many questions.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The AP and the Trump administration are due back in court in their fight over White House access
item.description: The Associated Press and Trump administration are due back in court in their fight over access to presidential events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump administration to end funding for vaccinating children in poor countries
item.description: Democrats say the list originated from the desk of Peter Marocco, one of the leading figures dismantling USAID alongside Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Judge says Justice Department attacked her character to 'impugn the integrity' of US judicial system
item.description: A federal judge accused the Justice Department of attacking her character in an effort to undermine the integrity of the judicial system, forcefully pushing back against the Trump administration’s criticism of the courts for blocking parts of the president’s agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Congressional Republicans target PBS, NPR funding in contentious hearing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A House Republican pushing the Trump administration's government efficiency efforts called for dismantling and defunding the nation's public broadcasting system following a contentious hearing Wednesday featuring the heads of PBS and NPR.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration says it will pull back billions in COVID funding from local health departments
item.description: Federal health officials said Tuesday they are pulling back $11.4 billion in COVID-19-related funds for local public health departments and other health organizations throughout the nation. Minnesota stands to lose $226 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: More Americans say NPR, PBS should be federally funded than not: Survey
item.description: The same survey found 32 percent of Democrats say they regularly get news from NPR, vs. 9 percent of Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: House Republican: White House ‘in denial’ about Signal war plans chat
item.description: Rep. Don Bacon (R-Neb.) said the White House is “in denial” over The Atlantic’s explosive report about a text chain with senior-level administration officials — and, inadvertently, a reporter — that discussed plans for a U.S. attack on the Houthis in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements for buying the kits online.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayoral poll: Cuomo grows lead, Mamdani gains traction
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo is still holding onto the lead in the New York City mayoral race – but one other candidate is also gaining support, according to new exclusive polling conducted by PIX11 News in partnership with Emerson College and The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Florida lawmakers defy DeSantis in rift over state budget
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Once floated as the heir to Donald Trump’s GOP, Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is used to getting his way in Tallahassee. He has wielded power like no other governor in the state’s recent history and enjoys a Republican supermajority in both chambers.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court blocks Trump admin's deportation flights in Alien Enemies Act immigration suit
item.description: A federal appeals court issued a decision in a high-profile immigration case challenging the Trump administration's authority to deport Venezuelan nationals via a 1798 wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: The Atlantic releases the Signal chat showing Hegseth's detailed attack plans against the Houthis
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Atlantic on Wednesday released the entire Signal chat among senior national security officials, showing that Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth provided the exact timings of warplane launches and when bombs would drop — before the men and women flying those attacks against Yemen’s
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What we know about Minnesota's government employee return to office plan
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz has directed workers to report to offices or job sites at least 50 percent of the time beginning in June. Employee unions are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Clay Masters, and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: California lawmaker revives effort to end forced prison labor
item.description: A new California bill seeks to prohibit the Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation from forcing inmates to work.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Fani Willis 'thinks she's above the law,' says Georgia lawmaker in subpoena fight
item.description: Georgia lawmakers deliberated Wednesday on how to press Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to comply with a committee subpoena.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blue state sanctuary laws enabled illegal 'abolish ICE' activist to evade capture, says local DA
item.description: Colorado District Attorney George Brauchler is speaking out against sanctuary policies in his state that he says enabled activist Jeanette Vizguerra to evade capture, “making a mockery of the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump will announce auto tariffs at a White House news conference
item.description: President Donald Trump will announce tariffs on auto imports, a move the White Houses claims would foster domestic manufacturing that could also put a financial squeeze on automakers that depend on global supply chains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NPR and PBS heads answer questions on Capitol Hill regarding allegations of bias
item.description: The CEOs of the largest U.S. public broadcasting networks appeared before a House subcommittee chaired by Republican Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman and David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Top agency staffer trying to block crucial Trump directive once managed DEI team
item.description: Republicans are fuming that the GAO's general counsel who shut down President Trump's request to supersede California's gas emission laws once managed DEI initiatives for the agency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats take hope from upset win in a GOP-leaning Pennsylvania state Senate district
item.description: Democrat James Andrew Malone has narrowly won a special election for a Pennsylvania state Senate seat in a stretch of Republican-leaning suburbs and farming communities.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo And Marc Levy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gabbard calls Signal chats a 'mistake' as Trump officials face grilling over leaked military plan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard said Wednesday it was a “mistake” for national security officials to discuss sensitive military plans on a group text chain that also included a journalist — a leak that has roiled President Donald Trump's national security leadership
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for OK to cut teacher-training money as part of anti-DEI push
item.description: The Trump administration is at the Supreme Court with a new emergency appeal, this time seeking approval to go ahead with cuts of hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training.
item.dc:creator: MPR News
